definitions: An economic strategy adopted by a country or entity to withstand external pressures, sanctions, or blockades, often by promoting self-sufficiency, local production, and alternative trade routes.
A system designed to minimize vulnerability to foreign economic influence and maximize resilience in the face of adversity.
examples: The nation implemented a resistance economy to counter the sanctions.
Developing domestic industries is key to a resistance economy.
Experts debated the effectiveness of the resistance economy.
